description: verify CUDA / NVML access inside a running container by probing `nvidia-smi -L` via docker exec — detects the silent stale-GPU-handle state (container still 'Up' in docker ps but NVML init fails) before a config-apply / app-launch so the deploy can steer to a fresh restart
rules: read-only probe — runs `nvidia-smi -L` inside the container; submits no GPU work and mutates nothing
emits a parseable marker: GPU_OK <container> <gpu-line> when the GPU is visible, GPU_STALE <container> when NVML init failed
CONTAINER_NAME is required; degrades to a GPU_PROBE_SKIPPED note when docker / the container / the GPU is unavailable
fast (~0.5s) so it can gate the Step 3 container-reuse decision
usage: Set CONTAINER_NAME (the running perception container, e.g. rtvicv-perception-docker). Output: gpu-probe.txt (GPU_OK / GPU_STALE / GPU_PROBE_SKIPPED marker plus diagnostics) under record_store.
limitation: Requires Docker, a running container of that name, and an NVIDIA GPU + driver visible inside it; without them the perk writes a GPU_PROBE_SKIPPED note and exits 0 rather than a real verdict.
